raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX, someone in XXXX ( where I haven't been this year ) charged {$460.00} on my LLBean card. When I got the bill, I immediately alerted them to the fraud and they removed the charges and sent a new card. Next I got a bill for {$14.00}, which I told them on XX/XX/XXXX were also part of the fraud, as these were " foreign transaction fees. '' After I got another bill for that amount, I called the fraud department directly on XX/XX/XXXX and spoke with someone who told me the fraud on my account was still under investigation. On XX/XX/XXXX, I got a bill for {$530.00} for the fraudulent amounts, plus late fees and interest. I mailed a letter detailing these events and saying that if this isn't resolved, I would contact the CFPB or any other agency necessary. The following day, I received a letter telling me that they decided this was not fraud since the card was used at a chip enabled system. I don't know how that's possible, as I had the card with me and was not in XXXX. I have never used the new card they sent me. I have put a fraud alert on XXXX, etc., and I have registered with IdentityTheft.gov.
